rulings.Case Title: IN RE: SAN FRANCISCO HOUSING AUTHORITY
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Hearing Of Verified Petition Of Sfha For An Order To Perpetuate Testimony
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Thursday, October 16, 2014, Line 1,PETITIONER SAN FRANCISCO HOUSING AUTHORITY's Verified Petition Of Sfha For An Order To Perpetuate Testimony The motion, which is made pursuant to sections of the California Discovery Act, is continued to October 30, 2014, to be heard on the discovery calendar. The matter will be heard at 9:00 a.m. in room 301 on October 30, 2014. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number.=(302/MJM)

rulings.Case Title: IN RE: CSAA INSURANCE GROUP
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion For Discovery Of Police Offier'S Personnel Records
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, October 16, 2014, Line 1, PETITIONER CSAA INSURANCE GROUP's Motion For Discovery Of Police Offier'S Personnel Records. Pro Tem Judge Phil Andersen, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: The parties should appear for the hearing. No tentative ruling. The opposition should be emailed to Philip Andersen by 4:00 p.m. on October 15, 2014 Philip.andersen.nx3z@statefarm.com. If the opposition is not received by 4:00 p.m. the matter will be continued to a date to be set by the court. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: MATTHEW WANG DBA CTW MECHANICAL VS. GREEN APPLE, INC., A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT/DEFAULT JUDGMENT/LEAVE TO DEFEND
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Thursday, October 16, 2014, Line 5, DEFENDANT GREEN APPLE, INC., A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION's MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT/DEFAULT JUDGMENT/LEAVE TO DEFEND The motion to set aside default and default judgment is granted for good cause shown pursuant to CCP 473(b). The motion was filed less than 6 months after default judgment against defendant. Defendant has established through the declarations of Ken Ung, Janice Wong, and Tony C. Hoh that its failure to respond timely to the Complaint is due to mistake, inadvertence, and excusable neglect; and that the motion was made within a reasonable time under all of the circumstances. Defendant shall file the proposed Answer within ten days after notice of entry of this order. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4:00 p.m. stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if in pro per on the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested.=(302/MJM)

rulings.Case Title: CHUCK AMITAL, SUCCESSOR TRUSTEE OF THE FRED S. VS. NORMANDY APARTMENTS, A CALIFORNIA GENERAL et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Mtn To Compel Responses To Pltf'S First Req For Production Of Docs
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, October 16, 2014, Line 5 - PLAINTIFF CHUCK AMITAL, SUCCESSOR TRUSTEE's Motion To Compel Responses To Plaintiff'S First Request For Production Of Documents. Pro Tem Judge Phil Andersen, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: No tentative ruling. The parties should appear for the hearing. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: HEVERSON COSTA VS. STARLITE ELECTRIC, INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO CONSOLIDATE ACTIONS OF CASE(S) CGC-14-538731 WITH CASE CGC-14-538172
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Thursday, October 16, 2014, Line 6, DEFENDANTS STARLITE ELECTRIC, INC. and CHIK SZE's MOTION TO CONSOLIDATE ACTIONS OF CASE(S) CGC-14-538731 WITH CASE CGC-14-538172 The motion to consolidate for all purposes is granted, for good cause shown. A copy of this order is to be filed in both actions. The lead case number is CGC-14-538172. Parties are ordered to meet and confer to coordinate discovery to avoid repetition. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested.=(302/MJM)

rulings.Case Title: COLETTE HUGHES VS. KEVIN WALLACE et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Thursday, October 16, 2014, Line 7, DEFENDANTS WALLACE REMODELING, INC. and KEVIN WALLACE's DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT The demurrer to the First Amended Complaint ("FAC") is overruled on all grounds. As to causes of action 1-3 and 6, the FAC states facts sufficient to constitute a cause of action against Defendants; each adequately pleads the statutory elements of these causes of action. Each also adequately pleads who, what, when, and how the alleged misrepresentations were made. (CCP 430.10(e).) The demurrer to the 3rd cause of action on the ground that it is defective as to form is overruled. The demurrer to the 4th cause of action is overruled; it alleges facts sufficient to constitute a cause of action for professional negligence. The demurrers on ground of uncertainty are overruled. (CCP 430.10(f).) As to causes of action 1-4 and 6, Defendants have not addressed in their memorandum of points and authorities how these causes of action are uncertain. The title of the 5th cause of action does not make that cause of action uncertain and the demurrer that it fails to state a cause of action is also overruled. The 6th cause of action states a cause of action; a demurrer will not lie to attack a claim for a damage amount requested in a complaint. The Court notes that the motion does not comply with Local Rule 2.6(c) ("if a motion challenges the sufficiency of a pleading¿moving party must also supply a courtesy copy of that pleading"). The Court did not deny the motion on this ground. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested.=(302/MJM)

rulings.Case Title: COLETTE HUGHES VS. KEVIN WALLACE et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Thursday, October 16, 2014, Line 7, DEFENDANTS WALLACE REMODELING, INC. and KEVIN WALLACE's MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT Denied without prejudice. Moving party failed to comply with Rule of Court 3.1322 ("A notice of motion to strike a portion of a pleading must quote in full the portions sought to be stricken except where the motion is to strike an entire paragraph cause of action, count or defense. Specifications in a notice must be numbered consecutively.") Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested.=(302/MJM)
